title: Almanach — SceneryStack Knowledge Base
description: A file-based knowledge base of SceneryStack knowledge: API guides, software patterns, styling, and accessibility conventions for building interactive simulations. Every page is plain Markdown with structured frontmatter.

documents.title: Deploying a Simulation to GitHub Pages
documents.description: Building a static SceneryStack simulation and publishing it to GitHub Pages via the gh-pages branch or GitHub Actions.

documents.title: Installation and Setup
documents.description: Setting up a new SceneryStack project - npm install, TypeScript config, and dev tooling.

documents.title: Project Structure Conventions
documents.description: Standard folder/file layout of a SceneryStack repo (model/view split, strings, images).

documents.title: Running and Building a Simulation
documents.description: Dev server workflow and production build output for a SceneryStack sim.

documents.title: Scenery Application vs. Standalone Library
documents.description: Choosing between a full joist-based Sim application and using scenery as a standalone rendering library.

documents.title: Supported Browsers
documents.description: Browser/platform support matrix and graceful-degradation considerations.

documents.title: Troubleshooting Common Setup Errors
documents.description: The most likely failure modes when starting a new scenerystack project - Node version mismatches, wrong import subpaths, missing peer deps, and TypeScript module resolution.

documents.title: What is SceneryStack?
documents.description: Overview of the SceneryStack framework, its constituent libraries, and how to install and import it.

documents.title: Your First Simulation
documents.description: Scaffolding a minimal one-screen simulation with Sim, Screen, and ScreenView.

documents.title: Accessible Interaction Tutorial
documents.description: Making a custom draggable Node fully accessible end-to-end.

documents.title: Advanced Layout Techniques
documents.description: Nesting FlowBox/GridBox containers, escaping to ManualConstraint for one-off positioning, and using AlignGroup to size content consistently across containers.

documents.title: Building Your First Screen
documents.description: Step-by-step model/view/Screen wiring for a new screen in a multi-screen sim.

documents.title: Chart and Data Visualization with Bamboo
documents.description: Subsystem overview of bamboo - the ChartTransform every plot shares, the family of Plot Node types built on it, and axis/gridline decoration.

documents.title: Continuous Integration for SceneryStack Projects
documents.description: What a CI pipeline for a SceneryStack simulation repository typically checks - type-checking, linting, a production build, unit tests, and automated fuzzing.

documents.title: Debugging Common Scenery Rendering Issues
documents.description: A walkthrough of the most common visual bugs in a scenery scene graph - Nodes that won't show, layout that won't update, and z-order surprises - and the debug tools that narrow each one down.

documents.title: Haptics and Alternative Feedback Channels
documents.description: Sound (tambo) and vibration (tappi) as feedback channels beyond the visual scene graph - when a simulation benefits from each, and how they relate to accessibility.

documents.title: Internationalization Deep Dive
documents.description: Runtime locale switching, right-to-left layout, and PatternStringProperty for pluralization and placeholders, building on the translation pipeline basics.

documents.title: Modifying SceneryStack
documents.description: Workflow for patching or contributing to the underlying common-code libraries.

documents.title: Performance and Profiling
documents.description: Finding and fixing slow rendering or update-loop performance in a SceneryStack simulation - profiling query parameters, Node/Property overhead, and Canvas/SVG/WebGL tradeoffs.

documents.title: PhET-iO and Instrumentation
documents.description: Conceptual guide to PhET-iO - what instrumentation buys you and how Tandem fits in.

documents.title: PhET-iO Deep Dive
documents.description: PhetioGroup and PhetioCapsule for dynamically created elements, authoring a custom IOType, and how PhET-iO state save/restore actually works.

documents.title: Preferences and Feature Flags
documents.description: Using the Preferences dialog and query parameters to configure sim behavior.

documents.title: Publishing and Distributing a Simulation
documents.description: What a SceneryStack production build produces and the common ways to host it - static hosting, GitHub Pages, or a project's own server.

documents.title: Scenery Basics
documents.description: Introduction to the scene graph - the Node tree, render layers, and coordinate frames.

documents.title: Scenery Input
documents.description: Overview of scenery's input system - pointers, listeners, and event phases.

documents.title: Scenery Layout
documents.description: Overview of scenery's auto-layout system - FlowBox, GridBox, AlignBox, and layout options.

documents.title: Sim Lifecycle and Startup Sequence
documents.description: The real sequence from Sim construction through start(), screen selection, and the running animation loop that steps Properties and listeners every frame.

documents.title: Testing and QA Strategy Overview
documents.description: The layers of testing a SceneryStack simulation benefits from - headless unit tests, manual QA passes, and automated fuzz-testing.

documents.title: Translation and Localization
documents.description: String management with StringProperty and the PhET translation pipeline.

documents.title: UI Components Overview
documents.description: Tour of the sun and scenery-phet component libraries and when to reach for each.

documents.title: Working with Model-View Transforms
documents.description: Why SceneryStack simulations separate model coordinates from view pixels, and the common ModelViewTransform2 usage patterns that follow from it.

documents.title: Working with Sound (tambo Overview)
documents.description: How tambo fits into a SceneryStack simulation - soundManager as the central mixer, and the sound-generator classes you compose to drive it.

documents.title: BooleanProperty
documents.description: A Property<boolean> with a toggle() convenience method.

documents.title: DerivedProperty
documents.description: A read-only Property computed from one or more dependencies.

documents.title: DerivedStringProperty
documents.description: A DerivedProperty specialized for building translated or formatted string values, with the right PhET-iO metadata baked in.

documents.title: DynamicProperty
documents.description: A Property that re-points itself to follow whichever inner Property a source Property currently holds.

documents.title: Emitter
documents.description: Discrete events that are not state.

documents.title: EnabledComponent and EnabledProperty
documents.description: The standard mixin-free pattern for giving any object a settable enabledProperty, with PhET-iO instrumentation baked in.

documents.title: EnumerationProperty
documents.description: A Property restricted to the values of an EnumerationValue subclass.

documents.title: Multilink
documents.description: Reacting to several Properties at once without manually chaining links.

documents.title: NumberProperty
documents.description: A Property<number> with an optional range for validation and UI binding.

documents.title: Observable Array
documents.description: createObservableArray: a Property-like array with add/remove item emitters.

documents.title: PatternStringProperty
documents.description: A derived string Property that fills a translated pattern string's placeholders from other Properties or plain values.

documents.title: Property
documents.description: The base observable value wrapper underlying all reactive state.

documents.title: ReadOnlyProperty
documents.description: The base observable class Property extends — the public type exposed by anything that shouldn't be externally settable.

documents.title: StringProperty
documents.description: A Property<string>'s role in localizable, translatable values.

documents.title: StringUnionProperty
documents.description: A Property constrained to a fixed union of string literal values, with validValues and PhET-iO serialization set up automatically.

documents.title: Timer, animationFrameTimer, and stepTimer
documents.description: The shared Timer class and its two singleton instances — setTimeout/setInterval-alike scheduling driven by the sim's own frame loop instead of the browser clock.

documents.title: TinyEmitter
documents.description: The dependency-free, no-PhET-iO Emitter core that axon uses internally for hot paths.

documents.title: TinyProperty
documents.description: The minimal Property base, without phet-io instrumentation or validation, that Property and scenery internals build on.

documents.title: UnitConversionProperty
documents.description: A derived, bidirectional Property that converts a numeric Property's value (and range) between units via a fixed multiplicative factor.

documents.title: Validation and validate
documents.description: The shared validator schema and checking utility that Property's valueType/validValues/isValidValue options are built on.

documents.title: AreaPlot
documents.description: Renders a dataset as a filled, shaded region between the data and a baseline value on a bamboo chart.

documents.title: AxisLine and AxisArrowNode
documents.description: Draws a bamboo chart's x or y axis as a plain line or an arrow-tipped line, positioned and auto-hidden by a ChartTransform.

documents.title: BarPlot
documents.description: Draws one rectangular bar per data point on a bamboo chart, growing from a shared baseline value.

documents.title: CanvasLinePlot
documents.description: A Canvas-rendered equivalent of LinePlot, painted by a shared ChartCanvasNode for charts with too many points for per-point scenery Nodes to stay fast.

documents.title: ChartTransform
documents.description: Maps chart data between model coordinates and view (pixel) coordinates, and is the shared object every bamboo plot and axis is built around.

documents.title: GridLineSet, TickMarkSet, and TickLabelSet
documents.description: The three bamboo Path/Node sets that draw a chart's repeating gridlines, tick marks, and tick labels at a fixed model-coordinate spacing.

documents.title: LinearEquationPlot
documents.description: Draws the straight line y = mx + b (or a vertical line, for infinite slope) across a bamboo chart's model x-range.

documents.title: LinePlot
documents.description: Draws a polyline through a dataset of points on a bamboo chart, with null entries producing gaps.

documents.title: ScatterPlot
documents.description: Draws a dataset of points as filled circles on a bamboo chart.

documents.title: Bounds2
documents.description: An axis-aligned rectangle used for layout, drag bounds, and hit-testing.

documents.title: Bounds3
documents.description: An axis-aligned cuboid (3D bounding box), the 3D counterpart to Bounds2.

documents.title: Complex
documents.description: A complex number type with immutable and mutable arithmetic, including polar form and polynomial root-solving.

documents.title: ConvexHull2
documents.description: A Graham Scan implementation computing the 2D convex hull (minimal enclosing polygon) of a set of Vector2 points.

documents.title: DampedHarmonic
documents.description: A closed-form solver for the damped harmonic oscillator differential equation, useful for spring, pendulum, and oscillation sims.

documents.title: Dimension2
documents.description: A plain width/height pair, like Bounds2 without a position.

documents.title: dotRandom
documents.description: The shared, globally-seeded Random singleton simulations should use for every "random" call, instead of Math.random() or a private Random instance.

documents.title: LinearFunction
documents.description: A reusable object that maps values between two linear numeric domains, with an invertible mapping.

documents.title: Matrix Decompositions
documents.description: A practical survey of dot's LUDecomposition, QRDecomposition, EigenvalueDecomposition, and SingularValueDecomposition, and when a sim author would ever reach for one.

documents.title: Matrix3
documents.description: The 3x3 transform matrix behind Node transforms and ModelViewTransform2.

documents.title: Matrix4
documents.description: The 4x4 homogeneous transform matrix used for 3D affine math.

documents.title: Permutation and Combination
documents.description: Immutable combinatorics helpers for reordering (Permutation) or selecting a subset of (Combination) an array.

documents.title: Quaternion and Plane3
documents.description: A rotation quaternion class and an infinite-plane primitive used together for 3D rotation and plane/ray geometry.

documents.title: Random
documents.description: A seedable pseudo-random number generator class, plus the shared dotRandom singleton sims should use instead of Math.random().

documents.title: Range
documents.description: A min/max pair used for NumberProperty validation and slider ranges.

documents.title: RangeWithValue
documents.description: A Range subclass that also tracks a required default value within the min/max interval.

documents.title: Ray2
documents.description: A 2D ray (origin position plus a unit direction vector), used for hit-testing and intersection queries.

documents.title: Ray3
documents.description: A 3D ray (origin plus unit direction) used for picking and intersection queries in 3D-flavored code such as mobius.

documents.title: Transform3
documents.description: A cached, observable wrapper around a Matrix3, providing forward/inverse transform methods for points, deltas, bounds, and shapes.

documents.title: Utils
documents.description: dot's numeric helper functions: clamp, linear, roundSymmetric, and friends.

documents.title: Vector2
documents.description: The 2D vector/point class used throughout SceneryStack's math and positioning APIs.

documents.title: Vector2Property
documents.description: A Property<Vector2> subclass with built-in NaN and optional-bounds validation, the standard type for model positions.

documents.title: Vector3
documents.description: The 3D vector class used for color math and 3D-flavored simulations.

documents.title: Vector4
documents.description: The 4D vector class used for homogeneous coordinates and RGBA-style color math.

documents.title: CreditsNode
documents.description: Renders a simulation's credits (design, development, team, contributors, translation-independent thanks, etc.) as the content of the About dialog.

documents.title: HighlightNode
documents.description: An HBox of two gradient-faded vertical bars used as the press/hover highlight behind navigation bar buttons.

documents.title: HomeScreen
documents.description: The auto-created Screen showing icon buttons for every screen in a multi-screen Sim.

documents.title: JoistButton
documents.description: The base class behind chrome buttons like HomeButton - custom black/white highlighting instead of the standard sun button look.

documents.title: LookAndFeel
documents.description: The Sim-wide manager of the current screen's background color and the derived navigation-bar colors that keep it readable against either black or white.

documents.title: NavigationBar
documents.description: The bar of screen buttons, title, and PhET menu shown at the bottom of every running Sim.

documents.title: PreferencesModel and PreferencesDialog
documents.description: PreferencesModel declares which Preferences tabs a Sim supports; PreferencesDialog is the tabbed dialog Sim builds automatically from it.

documents.title: PreferencesPanel, PreferencesPanelSection, PreferencesControl, PreferencesTabs, and PreferencesTab
documents.description: The building blocks the Preferences Dialog is assembled from, and the two of them (PreferencesPanelSection, PreferencesControl) sim authors use to build custom Preferences content.

documents.title: Region, Culture, and Locale Selection UI
documents.description: The runtime language/locale Property, the region-and-culture Property and its localized-image helper, and the Preferences UI built on top of both.

documents.title: Screen
documents.description: Pairing a model factory and view factory into one selectable unit of a Sim.

documents.title: ScreenIcon
documents.description: A Node wrapper that centers and scales an icon onto a fixed-size background, for use as a Screen's homeScreenIcon or navigationBarIcon.

documents.title: ScreenView
documents.description: The root Node of a Screen's view, with layoutBounds and visibleBoundsProperty.

documents.title: Sim
documents.description: The top-level application object owning Screens, the navigation bar, and the sim loop.

documents.title: SimInfo
documents.description: A snapshot of runtime/browser/simulation diagnostic data, built once per Sim and surfaced via the About dialog and PhET-iO data stream.

documents.title: Arc
documents.description: A circular arc segment defined by center, radius, and start/end angles — one of the pieces a Shape's subpaths are built from.

documents.title: Cubic
documents.description: A cubic Bezier curve segment defined by a start point, two control points, and an end point.

documents.title: EllipticalArc
documents.description: A generalization of Arc that sweeps an ellipse (independent x/y radii, plus rotation) rather than a circle.

documents.title: Graph and Boolean Shape Operations
documents.description: The Graph machinery powering Shape.union/intersection/xor and clipping — how to combine two Shapes, at a practical level.

documents.title: Line
documents.description: kite's straight-line path segment (pure geometry, not a renderable Node) — one of the pieces a Shape's subpaths are built from.

documents.title: LineStyles
documents.description: The stroke-rendering options (width, cap, join, dash, miter limit) used to turn a Shape's outline into a stroked shape.

documents.title: Quadratic
documents.description: A quadratic Bezier curve segment defined by a start point, one control point, and an end point.

documents.title: RayIntersection and SegmentIntersection
documents.description: The two small result types returned by kite's ray-casting and segment/segment intersection queries.

documents.title: Segment
documents.description: The abstract base class Arc, Cubic, Quadratic, Line, and EllipticalArc all extend — the shared parametric contract every segment type implements.

documents.title: Shape
documents.description: kite's path-description class behind Path, and its common construction/query methods.

documents.title: Subpath
documents.description: An ordered, connected run of segments within a Shape — the container between individual segments and the whole Shape.

documents.title: SVG Path Parsing and Serialization
documents.description: How Shape parses an SVG path-data string into segments, serializes back out with getSVGPath(), and the svgNumber/svgPath exports underneath both directions.

documents.title: ThreeIsometricNode
documents.description: A scenery Node that fills a ScreenView's layout bounds with an isometric-scaled three.js viewport for full-screen 3D content.

documents.title: ThreeNode, ThreeInstrumentable, and ThreeObject3DPhetioObject
documents.description: The scenery Node that hosts a fixed-size three.js viewport, and the mixin/PhetioObject pair used to give individual three.js objects a PhET-iO address.

documents.title: ThreeStage
documents.description: The three.js scene, camera, and WebGLRenderer that every mobius 3D view is built around.

documents.title: ThreeUtils
documents.description: Static helpers for converting between dot and three.js vector types, building quad geometry, loading textures, and detecting WebGL support.

documents.title: Atom, Element, and AtomNode
documents.description: The model layer for a single atom - immutable per-element physical constants (Element), a lightweight per-instance wrapper (Atom), and its shaded-sphere view (AtomNode).

documents.title: ChemUtils
documents.description: Plain-object chemistry helpers for molecular formulas, Hill-system sorting, and HTML subscript formatting in nitroglycerin sims.

documents.title: MoleculeNode
documents.description: The base class every pre-built molecule Node (H2ONode, CO2Node, and ~40 others) extends, composing a fixed arrangement of AtomNodes centered at the origin.

documents.title: arrayRemove, arrayDifference, and cleanArray
documents.description: Small, dependency-free array helpers for mutation-in-place, set-style comparison, and resetting an array without reallocating it.

documents.title: assertHasProperties and assertMutuallyExclusiveOptions
documents.description: Two development-time assertion helpers for validating shape - that an object has certain members, and that certain option keys are never combined.

documents.title: EnumerationValue
documents.description: The base class instances of a PhET-style closed enumeration extend, providing name/enumeration bookkeeping once paired with Enumeration.

documents.title: optionize and merge
documents.description: The two building blocks behind the options pattern - optionize for type-checked defaulting, merge for its untyped runtime engine.

documents.title: Orientation
documents.description: The HORIZONTAL/VERTICAL enumeration threaded through layout code (FlowBox, sliders, bamboo charts) to write orientation-agnostic logic.

documents.title: platform
documents.description: A plain object of boolean browser/OS/engine feature-detection flags, computed once from navigator.userAgent at import time.

documents.title: Pool
documents.description: A generic object-pooling utility that reuses instances instead of constructing new ones, reducing GC churn for objects created and discarded at high frequency.

documents.title: Bucket
documents.description: The model for a container UI element that other model objects can be dropped into and stacked within, as used in build-a-molecule-style sims.

documents.title: Fraction
documents.description: An exact-arithmetic numerator/denominator fraction class with add/subtract/multiply/divide and reduction, used throughout PhET's fraction and ratio sims.

documents.title: ModelViewTransform2
documents.description: Mapping between model coordinates (physical units, y-up) and view coordinates (pixels, y-down) with ModelViewTransform2.

documents.title: StringUtils
documents.description: phetcommon's small collection of string-handling helpers for translated pattern strings and bidirectional (LTR/RTL) text safety.

documents.title: Query parameter schema conventions (QSMSchemaObject)
documents.description: The shape of an individual QueryStringMachine schema entry - required and optional fields per type, and the validation rules the parser enforces.

documents.title: QueryStringMachine.getAll
documents.description: Parsing every URL query parameter for a sim in one call, plus the related single-key and non-URL-string variants on the same object.

documents.title: AlignBox
documents.description: A single-child container for alignment and fixed-size placement within layouts.

documents.title: AlignGroup
documents.description: Coordinates multiple independent AlignBoxes so they all share one common size.

documents.title: AnimatedPanZoomListener
documents.description: A pan/zoom listener supporting trackpad, wheel, and keyboard gestures that animates a target Node toward its destination pan/scale.

documents.title: Circle
documents.description: A Path subclass for drawing circles by radius.

documents.title: Color
documents.description: Scenery's RGBA color object - construction from RGB, hex, or CSS keywords, and mutation/derivation methods used as a Node's fill or stroke.

documents.title: ColorProperty, ProfileColorProperty, and PaintColorProperty
documents.description: The Property-driven pattern for colors that can change (by color profile, by derivation from another paint, or just by being observable) instead of being fixed values.

documents.title: Display
documents.description: The root object that turns a Node tree into rendered/attached DOM output.

documents.title: DragListener
documents.description: The pointer-driven drag listener that most draggable Nodes are built on.

documents.title: Filter and the Node.filters visual effects
documents.description: The Filter base class and its named subtypes (Brightness, Contrast, Grayscale, Invert, Sepia, HueRotate, Saturate) applied via Node.filters.

documents.title: FlowBox
documents.description: A single-axis (row/column) auto-layout container.

documents.title: FocusManager
documents.description: The static class that tracks DOM focus, pointer focus, and reading-block focus across a scenery application.

documents.title: Font
documents.description: Scenery's immutable font-description object used by Text and RichText, and the lower-level class PhetFont builds on.

documents.title: GridBox
documents.description: A two-dimensional grid auto-layout container.

documents.title: HBox
documents.description: A FlowBox convenience subclass fixed to horizontal orientation, for laying children out in a row.

documents.title: Highlight Rendering: HighlightPath and HighlightOverlay
documents.description: The Path subclass and per-Display overlay that actually draw focus, interactive, and reading-block highlights.

documents.title: Hit-Testing and Picking
documents.description: How scenery decides what a pointer is over, and the Node options that shape it.

documents.title: Hotkey, HotkeyData, and globalHotkeyRegistry
documents.description: The declarative building blocks underneath KeyboardListener for defining keyboard shortcuts.

documents.title: HSeparator, VSeparator, HStrut, and VStrut
documents.description: Small layout-helper Nodes for adding a visible dividing line or invisible fixed-size gap inside a FlowBox/VBox/HBox.

documents.title: Image
documents.description: A Node that renders a raster image with optional Property-driven swapping.

documents.title: InteractiveHighlighting
documents.description: The trait that shows a highlight on pointer hover for interactive Nodes, mirroring keyboard focus highlights.

documents.title: KeyboardDragListener
documents.description: The keyboard-driven equivalent of pointer dragging, moving a positionProperty with arrow keys or WASD.

documents.title: KeyboardListener
documents.description: A declarative hotkey listener that fires a callback when a specific combination of keys is pressed.

documents.title: Line
documents.description: A Path subclass for drawing single line segments.

documents.title: LinearGradient
documents.description: A paint type for straight-line color gradients, usable anywhere a Node accepts a fill or stroke.

documents.title: ManualConstraint
documents.description: A low-level layout constraint for one-off imperative positioning logic that should automatically rerun when the involved Nodes' bounds change.

documents.title: Node
documents.description: The base class of every item in the scene graph and its core options.

documents.title: ParallelDOM
documents.description: The trait every Node mixes in that provides its Parallel DOM options, such as tagName and accessibleName.

documents.title: Path
documents.description: A Node that renders a kite Shape with fill/stroke.

documents.title: Pattern
documents.description: The image-tiling paint type usable as a Node's fill or stroke - unrelated to the software-design conventions in the patterns/ section.

documents.title: Pointer, Mouse, Touch, and Pen
documents.description: The abstract input-device abstraction behind every scenery pointer event, and its Mouse/Touch/Pen subclasses.

documents.title: PressListener (direct usage)
documents.description: Using PressListener directly for custom pressable interactions, and the PDOM/focus-aware state it exposes beyond what FireListener and DragListener summarize.

documents.title: PressListener and FireListener
documents.description: The base classes behind clickable/pressable Nodes.

documents.title: RadialGradient
documents.description: A paint type for circular color gradients between two circles, usable anywhere a Node accepts a fill or stroke.

documents.title: Rectangle
documents.description: A Path subclass for drawing (optionally rounded) rectangles.

documents.title: RichDragListener
documents.description: A single input listener that composes a DragListener and a KeyboardDragListener, giving one draggable object both pointer and keyboard support.

documents.title: RichText
documents.description: A Text-like Node supporting a constrained markup subset for mixed styling.

documents.title: SceneryEvent
documents.description: The event object scenery passes to input listeners, wrapping the native DOM event, the pointer, and the hit trail.

documents.title: Sizable, WidthSizable, and HeightSizable
documents.description: The mixins that let a custom Node expose a settable preferred/minimum size so layout containers can resize it.

documents.title: Spacer
documents.description: An invisible, childless Node that reserves a fixed rectangular area of layout space.

documents.title: Sprites, Sprite, SpriteImage, and SpriteSheet
documents.description: The high-performance rendering system for drawing many instances of a small set of images in a single Node, instead of one scenery Node per instance.

documents.title: Text
documents.description: A Node that renders a single string of plain text.

documents.title: Trail
documents.description: An indexed path of Nodes from a root to a descendant, used for hit-testing, ordering, and coordinate transforms across the scene graph.

documents.title: TransformTracker
documents.description: Efficiently watches a Trail's cumulative transform for changes, without re-deriving it from scratch on every check.

documents.title: VBox
documents.description: A FlowBox convenience subclass fixed to vertical orientation, for stacking children in a column.

documents.title: Voicing
documents.description: The Voicing trait's class-level API - response Properties, speak methods, and how to compose it into a Node.

documents.title: WebGLNode, CanvasNode, and DOM
documents.description: The three alternate rendering-backend Node subclasses for custom drawing outside scenery's default SVG/Canvas pipeline, compared.

documents.title: ArrowNode
documents.description: A configurable arrow shape used for vectors, force diagrams, and callouts.

documents.title: BackButton
documents.description: A rectangular button with a leftward arrow icon, used for navigating back (e.g. to a level-selection screen).

documents.title: Background Nodes
documents.description: Small building blocks for scene backgrounds — the sky/ground gradient family (SkyNode, GroundNode, OutsideBackgroundNode) plus BackgroundNode and GradientRectangle.

documents.title: BannedNode
documents.description: The universal "no"/prohibition icon — a circle with a diagonal slash through it.

documents.title: BicyclePumpNode
documents.description: An animated bicycle-pump graphic whose handle, when dragged down, injects discrete units (particles, air, etc.) into a model quantity.

documents.title: BoxNode
documents.description: A pseudo-3D box drawn as three flat parallelograms (top, front, right side), used for capacitor and battery visualizations.

documents.title: BracketNode
documents.description: A curly-brace-like bracket Path with a single tip, optionally labeled, pointing in one of four directions.

documents.title: BucketFront and BucketHole
documents.description: The two view Nodes that render a phetcommon Bucket model — a hole behind draggable contents and a front in front of them.

documents.title: Capacitor Visualization Nodes
documents.description: The CapacitorNode family — a pseudo-3D capacitor visualization (plates, plate charge, and E-field lines) shared by Capacitor Lab and Circuit Construction Kit-style sims.

documents.title: CloseButton
documents.description: A red rectangular button with a white "X" icon, the standard control for dismissing a dialog or panel.

documents.title: ComboBoxDisplay
documents.description: A ComboBox whose items are live NumberDisplay readouts instead of static labels - pick which of several dynamic quantities to show.

documents.title: ConductivityTesterNode
documents.description: A light bulb wired to a battery and two draggable probes, visualizing circuit conductivity for chemistry sims.

documents.title: Developer Overlay Nodes
documents.description: CanvasWarningNode and PointerCoordinatesNode — two debug/developer-only overlay Nodes for surfacing a WebGL fallback warning and live pointer coordinates.

documents.title: Drawer
documents.description: A container that animates open and closed, revealing or hiding an arbitrary contents Node, toggled by a handle.

documents.title: EraserButton
documents.description: A standard rectangular button with an eraser icon, typically wired to a "clear" or "reset the drawing" action.

documents.title: EyeDropperNode
documents.description: An eye-dropper graphic with a squeeze-bulb button for dispensing colored fluid, driven by dispensing/empty Properties.

documents.title: FaceNode
documents.description: A simple smiling/frowning face graphic, typically shown as feedback for a correct or incorrect answer.

documents.title: FaucetNode
documents.description: A faucet control with a draggable pinball-shooter-style handle that drives a flow-rate Property, used in fluid/flow simulations.

documents.title: FineCoarseSpinner
documents.description: A five-part numeric spinner - fine decrement, coarse decrement, a NumberDisplay readout, coarse increment, fine increment.

documents.title: FormulaNode
documents.description: Renders a LaTeX-style math formula via KaTeX, as a DOM Node embedded in the scene graph.

documents.title: Fraction Display Nodes
documents.description: A small family of Nodes for rendering fractions, mixed numbers, and hand-drawn plus/minus signs without relying on Text glyphs.

documents.title: GaugeNode
documents.description: A circular dial gauge with a needle, tick marks, and a label, driven by a numeric value Property within a fixed range.

documents.title: GrabDragInteraction
documents.description: The accessible "grab with Enter/Space, arrow-key drag, release with Escape" interaction pattern that mixes into an existing draggable Node's PDOM content.

documents.title: GridCheckbox and GridIcon
documents.description: A Checkbox pre-built with a small NxN-grid icon, for toggling a coordinate grid's visibility.

documents.title: GridNode
documents.description: A Path drawing an evenly-spaced coordinate grid, positioned and re-projected via a ModelViewTransform2 Property.

documents.title: Group Sort Interaction
documents.description: The accessible keyboard pattern for selecting an item within a group and then sorting it by changing its value — GroupSelectModel/GroupSelectView for selection alone, GroupSortInteractionView adding sorting.

documents.title: HandleNode
documents.description: A purely decorative graphic of a grippable handle attached to a surface by one or two elbow-shaped mounts.

documents.title: HeaterCoolerNode
documents.description: A stove-style heating/cooling control driven by a bidirectional -1-to-+1 Property, with an embedded vertical slider.

documents.title: Icon Toggle Buttons
documents.description: SoundToggleButton, TimerToggleButton, and EyeToggleButton — toggle buttons bound to a boolean Property that swap between two pre-built icons.

documents.title: InfoButton
documents.description: The standard round grey button bearing an "i" info-circle icon, typically used to open an informational dialog.

documents.title: Keyboard Help Node Basics
documents.description: KeyNode and its subclasses render individual keycap icons; KeyboardHelpIconFactory composes them into the rows used by a Keyboard Help Dialog.

documents.title: Keyboard Help Sections
documents.description: KeyboardHelpSection and KeyboardHelpSectionRow lay out one labeled group of shortcuts; a family of ready-made sections covers sliders, spinners, time controls, combo boxes, and more.

documents.title: Keypad
documents.description: A calculator-like on-screen numeric keypad, driven by a pluggable key-accumulator that interprets key presses into a value.

documents.title: LaserPointerNode
documents.description: A laser-pointer-styled Node with an optional on/off button, used to represent a light source in optics sims.

documents.title: LightBulbNode
documents.description: A bulb image that glows and casts light rays as a function of a 0-1 brightness Property.

documents.title: LightRaysNode
documents.description: A Path of radiating line segments whose count and length scale with a 0-1 brightness value.

documents.title: LineArrowNode
documents.description: A lightweight, stroke-only arrow built from three line segments, as opposed to the filled-shape ArrowNode.

documents.title: LockNode
documents.description: A padlock icon that swaps between locked and unlocked graphics based on a boolean Property.

documents.title: MagnifyingGlassNode
documents.description: A simple magnifying-glass icon (circular glass plus a handle) that can center an arbitrary icon Node inside the glass.

documents.title: MathSymbolFont
documents.description: The italic serif font PhET sims use for math symbols and variables, kept visually distinct from PhetFont.

documents.title: MatrixNode
documents.description: Renders an MxN matrix of numbers or strings between bracket glyphs, in row-major order.

documents.title: MeasuringTapeNode
documents.description: A draggable measuring tape with a base, an extendable tip, and a live distance readout, common in physics sims.

documents.title: Movement and Border Alerters
documents.description: Alerter, MovementAlerter, and BorderAlertsDescriber — utilities that announce which direction a dragged object moved and when it has hit the edge of its movement bounds.

documents.title: NumberControl
documents.description: A composite slider + label + value-readout control bound to a NumberProperty.

documents.title: NumberDisplay
documents.description: A labeled, fixed-width numeric readout in a background rectangle, bound to a number Property.

documents.title: OffScaleIndicatorNode
documents.description: A labeled arrow-in-a-panel indicator that points toward data which has gone off the edge of a plot.

documents.title: PaperAirplaneNode and SimpleClockIcon
documents.description: Two small, static decorative icon Nodes — the PhET-logo paper airplane, and a plain analog clock face.

documents.title: ParametricSpringNode
documents.description: A coiled spring drawn from a parametric (cycloid-like) equation, with live-adjustable loop count, radius, and phase.

documents.title: PhetColorScheme, SceneryPhetColors, and SceneryPhetConstants
documents.description: The shared default colors and numeric constants reused across scenery-phet components, so common physics-quantity colors and button sizing stay consistent across sims.

documents.title: PhetFont
documents.description: The standard font class used across every SceneryStack simulation.

documents.title: PhetUnit
documents.description: A rich enumeration-style unit value (e.g. meters, newtons) that a Property can carry to drive automatic value-plus-units string formatting.

documents.title: Play Control Buttons
documents.description: PlayControlButton, PlayStopButton, RecordStopButton, and PlayPauseStepButtonGroup — the round toggle buttons and layout group behind time/recording controls.

documents.title: PlayPauseButton
documents.description: The round toggle button that starts and stops time in a simulation, swapping between a play triangle and a pause icon.

documents.title: ProbeNode
documents.description: A physical-looking sensor probe graphic — a circular sensing head on a handle — typically wired to a readout body via WireNode.

documents.title: ProtractorNode
documents.description: A draggable, rotatable protractor image for measuring angles, backed by an angleProperty.

documents.title: QuestionBar
documents.description: A StatusBar that floats a single bold framing question at the top of a screen.

documents.title: ResetAllButton
documents.description: The standard circular reset control wired to a model's reset() method.

documents.title: RulerNode
documents.description: A ruler graphic with major/minor tick marks and a units label, for measurement-style interactions.

documents.title: sceneryPhetQueryParameters and isResettingAllProperty
documents.description: scenery-phet's own demo-app query parameters, and a global TinyProperty flag that's true while any reset-all is in progress.

documents.title: ScientificNotationNode
documents.description: Renders a number Property as mantissa x 10^exponent, updating live as the value changes.

documents.title: Screen Summary Layout Nodes
documents.description: PlayAreaNode, ControlAreaNode, and ScreenSummaryNode — the standard PDOM structural sections ScreenView uses to organize a screen's accessible description tree.

documents.title: ShadedRectangle and ShadedSphereNode
documents.description: Two pseudo-3D shading primitives - a beveled rounded rectangle and a specular-highlighted sphere - built from gradients rather than images.

documents.title: ShadowText
documents.description: A Text node rendered twice - an offset dark copy behind a foreground copy - to fake a drop shadow.

documents.title: Small Icon Utility Buttons
documents.description: RefreshButton, RestartButton, ReturnButton, CameraButton, ClapperboardButton, and StarButton — one-shot push buttons distinguished mainly by a fixed icon.

documents.title: Sound Drag Listeners
documents.description: SoundDragListener, SoundKeyboardDragListener, and SoundRichDragListener — drop-in replacements for scenery's drag listeners that also play grab/release sounds.

documents.title: SpectrumNode
documents.description: A Node that rasterizes a value range into a horizontal color gradient bar via a client-supplied value-to-color function.

documents.title: SpinningIndicatorNode
documents.description: A ring of elements whose fill colors cycle to simulate spinning, for indicating indeterminate progress.

documents.title: StarNode and StarShape
documents.description: A five-pointed star that fills in from left to right by a 0-to-1 value, used as PhET's standard game score indicator.

documents.title: StatusBar
documents.description: The scenery-phet base class for a resizing bar pinned to the top of a screen, handling only geometry — subclasses decide what content goes in it.

documents.title: StepBackwardButton
documents.description: A round push button with a backward-step icon, used to rewind a paused simulation by one fixed increment.

documents.title: StepForwardButton
documents.description: A round push button with a forward-step icon, used to advance a paused simulation by one fixed increment.

documents.title: StopSignNode
documents.description: An octagonal red stop-sign icon with a white inner border and black outer border.

documents.title: StopwatchNode
documents.description: A draggable digital stopwatch bound to a Stopwatch model element.

documents.title: TemperatureAndColorSensorNode
documents.description: A composite Node pairing a ThermometerNode with a triangular color-swatch indicator, for sensors that report both a temperature and a sampled color.

documents.title: ThermometerNode
documents.description: A fluid-level thermometer visualization bound to a temperature Property.

documents.title: TimeControlNode
documents.description: The standard play/pause/step-forward control for time-based simulations.

documents.title: TimeSpeedRadioButtonGroup
documents.description: A vertical Aqua radio button group for selecting among the TimeSpeed enumeration values (SLOW, NORMAL, FAST).

documents.title: Trash Buttons
documents.description: MoveToTrashButton, MoveToTrashLegendButton, and TrashButton — the standard trash-can icon buttons, with and without a "move to" arrow.

documents.title: TriangleNode
documents.description: An equilateral or isosceles triangle Path, pointing in any of the four cardinal directions.

documents.title: ValueGaugeNode
documents.description: A GaugeNode subclass with a built-in, centered NumberDisplay showing the gauge's numeric value.

documents.title: ViewSynchronizer
documents.description: A small utility that keeps a container Node's children in sync with an array of model elements, via a view factory function.

documents.title: Wavelength and Spectrum Sliders
documents.description: SpectrumSliderThumb and SpectrumSliderTrack build a color-gradient Slider; WavelengthNumberControl and WavelengthSpectrumNode specialize that for visible-light wavelength; VisibleColor converts between wavelength and RGB.

documents.title: WireNode
documents.description: A cubic-bezier wire Path that continuously reconnects two moving points, typically a ProbeNode and its readout body.

documents.title: XNode
documents.description: A simple 'x' mark Node, drawn as a PlusNode rotated 45 degrees.

documents.title: YawPitchModelViewTransform3
documents.description: A pseudo-3D model-view transform that projects 3D model coordinates onto a 2D view plane using a fixed yaw/pitch/scale, for capacitor-style oblique visualizations.

documents.title: Zoom Button Groups
documents.description: ZoomButton, ZoomButtonGroup, PlusMinusZoomButtonGroup, and MagnifyingGlassZoomButtonGroup — the standard zoom-in/zoom-out button pair bound to a ranged numeric Property.

documents.title: ABSwitch
documents.description: A labeled two-option toggle switch bound to a Property over exactly two named values.

documents.title: AccessibleNumberSpinner
documents.description: The trait/mixin that gives NumberSpinner its keyboard and PDOM behavior, with press-and-hold repeat.

documents.title: AccessibleSlider
documents.description: The trait/mixin that gives Slider (and HSlider/VSlider) its keyboard and PDOM behavior.

documents.title: AccordionBox
documents.description: An expandable/collapsible titled container bound to an expandedProperty.

documents.title: AquaRadioButton
documents.description: A single flat, circular radio button with a label Node, the building block behind AquaRadioButtonGroup.

documents.title: AquaRadioButtonGroup
documents.description: Aqua-style radio button groups bound to a Property, plus the Horizontal/VerticalAquaRadioButtonGroup orientation convenience subclasses.

documents.title: ArrowButton
documents.description: A small rectangular push button whose content is an arrow, with built-in press-and-hold repeat firing.

documents.title: Carousel
documents.description: A paged, scrollable strip of items with next/previous buttons, animated between pages.

documents.title: CarouselButton
documents.description: The flat-styled next/previous arrow button Carousel builds and manages internally.

documents.title: CarouselComboBox
documents.description: A ComboBox-like dropdown whose popup is a scrollable Carousel, for item lists too long for a plain list box.

documents.title: Checkbox
documents.description: A checkbox bound to a BooleanProperty, with label-Node composition.

documents.title: ColorConstants and SunConstants
documents.description: The small set of shared default colors and constant values reused across sun's UI components.

documents.title: ComboBox
documents.description: A dropdown selector bound to a Property over an enumerated set of items.

documents.title: ComboBoxListBox
documents.description: The popup list-box Panel that ComboBox creates internally to display its items.

documents.title: ExpandCollapseButton
documents.description: The square plus/minus toggle button that AccordionBox uses internally for its expand/collapse control.

documents.title: HSlider
documents.description: The horizontal slider bound to a NumberProperty and Range.

documents.title: MenuItem
documents.description: A single row in PhET's popup PhetMenu — a width-sizable, Voicing-enabled, optionally-checkable menu row.

documents.title: Momentary Buttons
documents.description: Press-and-hold buttons that set a Property to an "on" value only while pressed, reverting when released.

documents.title: NumberPicker
documents.description: A compact up/down arrow spinner bound to a NumberProperty, with its arrows drawn directly on the value display.

documents.title: NumberSpinner
documents.description: An integer value display flanked by increment/decrement arrow buttons, bound to a Property and a dynamic Range.

documents.title: OnOffSwitch
documents.description: The iOS-style sliding toggle for a boolean Property, one of three sun affordances for the same on/off pattern.

documents.title: PageControl
documents.description: An iOS-style row of dots indicating the current page, typically paired with Carousel.

documents.title: Panel
documents.description: A bordered/background container used to group related controls.

documents.title: PushButtonModel
documents.description: The non-visual press/fire state machine behind RectangularPushButton and RoundPushButton, and its ButtonModel base.

documents.title: Radio Button Groups
documents.description: Mutually-exclusive selection bound to a Property (rectangular and Aqua styles).

documents.title: RectangularButton
documents.description: The abstract rectangular button shape and appearance layer that every rectangular sun button builds on.

documents.title: RectangularPushButton
documents.description: The standard rectangular momentary-action button.

documents.title: RectangularRadioButton
documents.description: A single boxed radio button, the building block behind RectangularRadioButtonGroup.

documents.title: RoundButton
documents.description: The abstract round button shape and appearance layer that every circular sun button builds on.

documents.title: RoundPushButton
documents.description: The circular momentary-action button, sharing PushButtonModel with RectangularPushButton.

documents.title: Slider
documents.description: The orientation-agnostic base class behind HSlider and VSlider.

documents.title: SliderTrack, DefaultSliderTrack, and SliderThumb
documents.description: The pluggable track and thumb pieces you swap out to customize a Slider/HSlider/VSlider's visual appearance.

documents.title: Sticky Toggle Buttons
documents.description: Press-to-latch, press-again-to-release buttons bound to a two-value Property.

documents.title: TextPushButton
documents.description: A push button whose content is a text label.

documents.title: Toggle Buttons
documents.description: The rectangular/round toggle-button family bound to a BooleanProperty or two-state value.

documents.title: ToggleNode and BooleanToggleNode
documents.description: Show exactly one of several child Nodes, chosen by the current value of a Property.

documents.title: ToggleSwitch
documents.description: The generic two-value sliding switch that OnOffSwitch specializes to boolean on/off.

documents.title: VerticalCheckboxGroup
documents.description: A convenience VBox layout of several independent Checkboxes, one per boolean Property.

documents.title: VSlider
documents.description: The vertical counterpart to HSlider, bound to the same NumberProperty/Range pattern.

documents.title: How tambo's Sound Generation Fits Together
documents.description: A conceptual overview of how soundManager, SoundGenerator subclasses, and enabled-state gating compose into tambo's sonification model.

documents.title: PitchedPopGenerator
documents.description: A tambo sound generator that synthesizes a short "pop" sound at a controllable pitch, useful for sonifying discrete value changes.

documents.title: SoundClip
documents.description: A tambo sound generator that plays a decoded audio buffer as a one-shot or looping clip.

documents.title: SoundClipPlayer
documents.description: A lightweight, self-registering wrapper around SoundClip for sounds shared across many places in a sim.

documents.title: soundManager
documents.description: The singleton that registers tambo sound generators, routes them to the Web Audio output, and controls global enable/disable, per-category gain, and reverb.

documents.title: Built-in IOTypes
documents.description: A survey of tandem's ready-made IOType instances for JS primitives and common container shapes, and when to reach for one instead of writing a custom IOType.

documents.title: Disposable
documents.description: The base dispose-lifecycle class that PhetioObject (and therefore Node and every instrumented type) builds on.

documents.title: IOType
documents.description: Declares a class's PhET-iO serialization - state schema, methods, and documentation - independent of its runtime implementation.

documents.title: PhetioCapsule
documents.description: A PhET-iO-aware container for a single, lazily-created instrumented element, like an on-demand dialog.

documents.title: PhetioGroup
documents.description: A PhET-iO-aware container for a dynamically-growing/shrinking collection of instrumented elements, like electrons created during a sim.

documents.title: PhetioIDUtils and Tandem Naming Conventions
documents.description: Static string utilities for building and inspecting phetioIDs, and the naming conventions a tree of Tandems produces.

documents.title: PhetioObject
documents.description: The base class giving any object PhET-iO instrumentation - a tandem, an IOType, and metadata like phetioState/phetioReadOnly.

documents.title: Tandem
documents.description: The naming/instrumentation handle giving every PhET-iO-tracked element a stable tree address.

documents.title: VibrationIndicator and ContinuousPatternVibrationController
documents.description: An on-screen phone icon that visualizes vibration state, and a pattern-transition controller built on top of the (deprecated, iOS-only) native vibration bridge.

documents.title: vibrationManager and VibrationPatterns
documents.description: The shared singleton that drives device vibration through the Web Vibration API, plus a library of reusable on/off interval patterns to feed it.

documents.title: Animation and Easing
documents.description: Animating a Property's value smoothly over time instead of setting it instantly.

documents.title: AnimationTarget and Easing
documents.description: The per-value binding object and interpolation-curve class that Animation is built from internally, and how to use AnimationTarget directly for multi-target animations.

documents.title: DampedAnimation
documents.description: A single-dimension damped-harmonic-oscillator animation that pulls a value toward a target, as an alternative to Animation's fixed-duration easing curves.

documents.title: TransitionNode
documents.description: A Node that swaps between two child Nodes using an animated slide, wipe, or dissolve transition.

documents.title: Announcer, AriaLiveAnnouncer, and SpeechSynthesisAnnouncer
documents.description: The Announcer hierarchy that UtteranceQueue hands resolved alert text to — aria-live DOM updates and Web Speech API synthesis.

documents.title: ResponsePacket and ResponsePatternCollection
documents.description: Structuring a spoken response into name/object/context/hint categories, and controlling how those categories are combined into one string.

documents.title: Utterance
documents.description: A wrapper around alert content that tracks stability, priority, and per-feature announcing control before it reaches an UtteranceQueue.

documents.title: UtteranceQueue
documents.description: The FIFO queue that manages timing, priority, and interruption for Utterances before handing text off to an Announcer.

documents.title: AllLevelsCompletedNode
documents.description: A pseudo-dialog Node shown when a player has completed every level in a game, with a smiley face and a Done button.

documents.title: ElapsedTimeNode
documents.description: An HBox that displays a clock icon next to a formatted H:MM:SS (or M:SS) elapsed-time readout, driven by a numeric Property.

documents.title: FiniteStatusBar and InfiniteStatusBar
documents.description: The standard game-screen status bars that pin a score display (and optionally level/challenge/timer info) to the top of a ScreenView.

documents.title: GameAudioPlayer
documents.description: A small helper class that plays the standard set of vegas game-feedback sounds (correct/wrong answer, challenge complete, game-over variants).

documents.title: GameTimer
documents.description: A simple wall-clock-driven timer, ticking once per second, used to drive a game level's elapsed-time display.

documents.title: LevelCompletedNode
documents.description: A Panel shown when a player finishes a single game level, summarizing score, stars, and (optionally) elapsed/best time.

documents.title: LevelSelectionButton
documents.description: A push button for a game's level-selection screen, combining an icon, a score display, and a level-specific sound.

documents.title: LevelSelectionButtonGroup
documents.description: Instantiates and lays out a row (or custom arrangement) of LevelSelectionButtons from a declarative list of items, with built-in support for a gameLevels query parameter.

documents.title: ScoreDisplay Family
documents.description: The three sibling score-display Nodes alongside ScoreDisplayNumberAndStar -- a plain labeled number, a labeled star row, and a bare star row.

documents.title: ScoreDisplayNumberAndStar
documents.description: A compact vegas score display showing a number and a single star, used where space is tight (e.g. inside a LevelSelectionButton).

documents.title: Avoiding Property Leaks
documents.description: Listener-unlinking discipline for Properties, DerivedProperties, and Multilinks so dynamically created objects don't leak their whole dependency chain.

documents.title: Command/Undo Pattern for Interactive Sims
documents.description: Structuring a reversible user action as a command object with do/undo so a simulation can support undo, if it needs to - built on the same Property-based state that reset() and dispose() already rely on.

documents.title: Composing View Nodes
documents.description: Building a screen's view as small, composed Node subclasses instead of one giant ScreenView.

documents.title: Constants File Pattern
documents.description: The convention of one MySimConstants.ts module holding every shared magic number, color, and font - the same convention scenery-phet/sun's own SceneryPhetConstants and SunConstants follow.

documents.title: Derived-Enablement Pattern
documents.description: Computing a control's enabledProperty from a DerivedProperty over other model state, instead of imperatively setting .enabled from scattered call sites.

documents.title: Dispose and Memory Management
documents.description: The dispose() convention - when Nodes/Properties/listeners must clean up.

documents.title: Drag Listeners
documents.description: Making nodes draggable with DragListener, KeyboardDragListener, and RichDragListener, wired to a model positionProperty through a transform.

documents.title: Emitter vs. Property
documents.description: When to model state as a Property versus a discrete event as an Emitter.

documents.title: Feature Flags and Preferences Pattern
documents.description: The narrow convention of gating an in-development or optional feature behind a single, consistently-named boolean Property.

documents.title: Grid-Snapping and Constrained-Dragging Pattern
documents.description: When and why to constrain a draggable Node's input - snapping to a grid, clamping to bounds, or restricting to one axis - as distinct decisions layered on top of the ordinary drag-listener pattern.

documents.title: Immutable vs. Mutable Model Data
documents.description: When model state should be an immutable value object replaced wholesale via property.value = newValue, versus mutated in place - and why SceneryStack's math types default to returning new instances.

documents.title: Lazy Initialization Pattern
documents.description: Deferring an expensive object's construction until it's actually needed, instead of paying its cost unconditionally at startup.

documents.title: Model-View Separation
documents.description: The foundational SceneryStack architecture - axon Properties form the model, scenery Nodes observe it, and nothing flows the other way.

documents.title: Multi-Screen Sim Structure
documents.description: Structuring model/view code across multiple Screens in one Sim.

documents.title: PhET-iO Instrumentation Pattern
documents.description: Deciding what to instrument with Tandem and structuring a PhET-iO-ready model.

documents.title: Query Parameters Pattern
documents.description: Using query parameters to drive optional/debug behavior.

documents.title: Singleton and Namespace Conventions
documents.description: When a library exposes a ready-made shared-instance singleton (like dotRandom or animationFrameTimer) versus when you construct and own your own instance.

documents.title: State Persistence and Save/Restore Patterns
documents.description: Structuring model state as enumerable Properties so it can be saved and restored, whether by PhET-iO or by a simulation's own save/load feature.

documents.title: Testing Model Logic Headlessly
documents.description: Unit-testing model classes without instantiating scenery Nodes or a Display, so tests run fast and in plain Node.js.

documents.title: The Enumeration Pattern
documents.description: Modeling closed sets of values instead of string literals.

documents.title: The Multilink Pattern
documents.description: Combining multiple Properties into one derived reaction.

documents.title: The Options Pattern
documents.description: The options-object convention for configuring Nodes and components.

documents.title: The Reset-All Pattern
documents.description: Wiring every model Property's reset() through one ResetAllButton.

documents.title: Animation and Motion Design Conventions
documents.description: When to animate a transition instead of snapping it instantly, how long an animation should run, which Easing curve to reach for, and how to avoid overusing motion.

documents.title: Color Profiles
documents.description: Centralizing colors with ProfileColorProperty so an application can switch between default and projector color schemes at runtime.

documents.title: High-Contrast / Projector-Mode Deep Dive
documents.description: Palette-design considerations specific to projector mode - contrast ratios, saturation, and what breaks on a washed-out classroom projector.

documents.title: Icon Sizing and Button Padding Standards
documents.description: Standardizing icon dimensions and button touch-target padding once you already have icons and buttons, so a sim's controls read as one consistent set rather than a collection of individually-reasonable sizes.

documents.title: Iconography Conventions
documents.description: Building consistent icons for buttons and controls.

documents.title: Layout Container Conventions
documents.description: Styling conventions when composing FlowBox/GridBox/AlignBox layouts.

documents.title: Panels and Backgrounds
documents.description: Conventions for grouping controls with Panel/AccordionBox.

documents.title: Responsive Layout Strategies
documents.description: How a ScreenView's fixed layoutBounds and scenery's layout containers together handle windows of different sizes and aspect ratios.

documents.title: Spacing and Sizing Constants
documents.description: Centralizing margins, spacing, and icon sizes in one constants file instead of hardcoding magic numbers throughout view code.

documents.title: Stroke and Line Styling
documents.description: Conventions for stroke width, dash patterns, and line joins/caps.

documents.title: Typography and Fonts
documents.description: Using PhetFont consistently and font-size/weight conventions.

documents.title: Visual Debugging Aids
documents.description: Using scenery's built-in debug query parameters to visualize hit areas, pointer areas, and rendering-block boundaries while tracking down layout and input problems.

documents.title: Accessible Descriptions for Charts and Graphs
documents.description: Writing PDOM description content for a bamboo chart that conveys the data's trend and key values, not just the fact that a chart exists.

documents.title: Alternative Input Overview
documents.description: How pointer, keyboard, and switch/AT input all route through the same listeners.

documents.title: Cognitive Accessibility Considerations
documents.description: Reducing cognitive load - clear language, consistent layout, and avoiding unnecessary complexity or timers - as an accessibility axis distinct from screen-reader and keyboard support.

documents.title: Describing Dynamic State
documents.description: Writing description content that updates as model state changes.

documents.title: Focus Highlights
documents.description: Customizing focus highlights for focusable Nodes.

documents.title: Internationalized Accessible Names
documents.description: Routing accessibleName and other PDOM strings through the same StringProperty translation pipeline as visible text, instead of hardcoding them in English.

documents.title: Keyboard Input and Hotkeys
documents.description: KeyboardListener and Hotkey for custom keyboard shortcuts beyond dragging.

documents.title: Mobile Screen-Reader Testing Notes
documents.description: Practical notes on testing VoiceOver (iOS) and TalkBack (Android) with a sim, and the specific ways mobile screen-reader testing differs from testing on desktop.

documents.title: Screen-Reader Testing Checklist
documents.description: A practical checklist for manually testing a sim's PDOM with a real screen reader instead of relying on automated checks alone.

documents.title: Sound Design
documents.description: Using scenery-phet/tambo sound generators for non-speech audio feedback.

documents.title: The Parallel DOM (PDOM)
documents.description: How scenery renders an invisible, semantic HTML tree alongside the canvas so screen readers and keyboards can use the application.

documents.title: Voicing
documents.description: Speech-synthesis descriptions triggered by interaction, layered on the PDOM.

documents.title: A 3D Scene with Mobius
documents.description: A worked example setting up a basic mobius 3D scene and camera two ways - a full-screen isometric stage via MobiusScreenView, and a fixed-size viewport via ThreeNode - built entirely from already-documented mobius classes.

documents.title: A Chart-Driven Simulation with Bamboo
documents.description: A worked mini-sim where a model Property drives a live bamboo LinePlot through a ChartTransform, with an accessible description that tracks the trend.

documents.title: A Full Accessible Multi-Screen Sim
documents.description: Threading PDOM, keyboard, and Voicing support through every screen of a multi-screen sim from the first line of code, instead of retrofitting it later.

documents.title: A Game-Style Sim with Vegas Scoring
documents.description: A worked example combining vegas's LevelSelectionButton, ScoreDisplayNumberAndStar, GameAudioPlayer, and AllLevelsCompletedNode into a minimal level-selection and gameplay screen.

documents.title: A PhET-iO State-Restorable Sim
documents.description: A worked example that goes past basic instrumentation to a model whose ENTIRE state - including a runtime-created list of elements via PhetioGroup - can be saved and restored by PhET-iO with no bespoke save/restore code.

documents.title: A Sim Using Cookbook Recipes End-to-End
documents.description: A worked mini-scenario combining four cookbook recipes - a bounded draggable, grid snapping, a sound effect on interaction, and a hover tooltip - into one coherent grid-placement tool.

documents.title: A Sound-Rich Simulation with Tambo
documents.description: A worked example wiring SoundClip, PitchedPopGenerator, and sharedSoundPlayers to several distinct interaction points in one small sim.

documents.title: Accessible Control Panel Example
documents.description: A fully accessible control panel combining sun components with PDOM.

documents.title: Building a Two-Screen Simulation
documents.description: Full example wiring two Screens with shared and per-screen model code.

documents.title: Demo Simulation Walkthrough
documents.description: Annotated tour of a minimal end-to-end demo sim.

documents.title: Migrating a Legacy Sim to RichDragListener
documents.description: A before/after walkthrough of replacing a pointer-only DragListener with RichDragListener to add keyboard dragging without restructuring the model.

documents.title: PhET-iO Instrumented Sim Example
documents.description: A minimal simulation instrumented end-to-end with Tandem.

documents.title: Scenery Layout Examples
documents.description: Worked FlowBox/GridBox layouts for common UI arrangements.

documents.title: Standalone Scenery App Example
documents.description: Using scenery as a standalone rendering library outside the joist shell.

documents.title: Three.js Integration
documents.description: Embedding a Three.js WebGL scene inside a scenery Node.

documents.title: A Colorblind-Safe Palette
documents.description: Practical guidance and a worked ProfileColorProperty palette that stays distinguishable under common color-vision deficiencies.

documents.title: A Custom Focus Highlight Shape
documents.description: Overriding a Node's default rectangular focus highlight with a custom kite Shape via HighlightPath.

documents.title: A Custom Slider Thumb and Track
